name: PowerShell & Shell Usage Patterns
description: Standardized patterns for executing commands, managing environments, and handling filesystem operations in the Antigravity Agent Factory Windows/PowerShell environment.

python_execution.description: Consistent Python execution using the project's specific conda environment.
python_execution.note: Avoid using 'python' or 'python3' directly as it may resolve to the wrong system interpreter.

command_chaining.description: Executing multiple commands sequentially in a single run_command call.
command_chaining.note: In PowerShell, use the semicolon (;) for chaining.

path_handling.description: Robust path referencing to avoid 'file not found' errors.
path_handling.best_practice: Use absolute paths or paths relative to the repository root (d:\Users\wpoga\Documents\Python Scripts\antigravity-agent-factory).
path_handling.normalization: Normalize slashes to backslashes (\) for native Windows commands, though PowerShell handles forward slashes (/) for many operations.

best_practices: Always wrap paths with spaces in double quotes.
Use -Force for deletions to bypass confirmation prompts.
Explicitly reconfigure encoding if printing non-ASCII characters to the terminal (sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8')).
Prefer 'conda run' for script execution to ensure the environment is correctly activated including environment variables.
anti_patterns: Using '&&' for chaining (this is cmd.exe syntax, not native to PowerShell without specific wrappers).
Assuming the existence of Unix utilities like 'rm', 'mv', 'cp', or 'fd' unless verified via Get-Command.
Using relative paths like './' without being certain of the Current Working Directory (CWD).
